cards.title: Types of Claims
cards.content:
ΓÇó Survival Benefit: Paid periodically in Money Back policies.
ΓÇó Maturity Claim: Paid at the end of the term if the insured survives.
ΓÇó Death Claim: Paid to the nominee upon the death of the insured.

cards.title: Early vs Non-Early Claims
cards.content:
ΓÇó Early Death Claim: Death happens within 3 years of policy start or revival. (Always heavily investigated for fraud).
ΓÇó Non-Early Claim: Death after 3 years. Section 45 applies, making it indisputable.

items.text: The nominee informs the insurer about the death and requests a claim form.
Submission of Death Certificate, Original Policy, completed Claim Form, and KYC.
Insurer must process the claim within 30 days of receiving all documents.

sections.title: ≡ƒÆí Exam Master Tips
items: Presumption of Death: Under the Indian Evidence Act, a person missing for 7 years is presumed dead (court order needed).
Discharge Voucher: A receipt signed by the claimant confirming they have received the money.
If the nominee is a minor, the claim is paid to the Appointee.
If the insurer delays claim payment beyond 30 days, they must pay interest at Bank Rate + 2%.
For accidental death, FIR, Panchnama, and Post-Mortem reports are mandatory.
